story: On April Fools’ Day, Ted and I tucked Ent, Ted’s white mouse, into the shallow front drawer of his teacher Sharon’s desk, expecting her to sit and find him soon. That morning, Sharon had planned to stay on her feet for back-to-back activities and only sit just before lunch to grade papers. First period passed; she never sat. Second period too. By recess we were sick with worry about Ent.
implausible_knowledge: That morning, Sharon had planned to stay on her feet for back-to-back activities and only sit just before lunch to grade papers.
question: When recess begins and Sharon still hasn’t sat down, what will Ted most likely do?
